summary: DHH asks his blog readers for travel advice about planning a summer vacation to Hawaii. He mentions being busy with travel and wanting to lock down plans before prices rise and his calendar fills up. The post is a simple crowd-sourcing request for tips on enjoying Maui over 10-14 days in mid-August on a moderate budget.
key_points: DHH is planning a 10-14 day Hawaii trip around mid-August, likely Maui
He sets a modest hotel budget of ~$120/day, reflecting pre-Rails-fame frugality
He leverages his blog audience for travel recommendations, having done so successfully before
money_quotes: What's the best way to enjoy Hawaii?
Before ticket prices start to become filthy expensive and before my calendar books full.
key_insight: A brief, personal travel-advice request with no tech or business substance — a snapshot of early-era blogging when personal and professional content freely mixed.
